The difference between a copywriter that gets a hundred thousand dollar fee and a copywriter that gets a thousand dollar fee is not a hundred times more capability. There's more capability in most cases, but not by that multiple. It's what business they actually understand that they are in. And so being in the you business, now however you wish to describe it, self-aggrandizement is part of it. Really being in the status business is a transcendental factor in income. The big money gravitates to people of high perceived status. They hand over a lot more money to them than anybody else in a given category. And it was, I mean, we later, Gary and I had several conversations about it actually because he didn't really mean it as a teaching point, I don't think. And he obviously didn't spend any time on it. But I mean, it's a developable idea, if there is such a word, that really when you rethink what business you're in, you know, we teach to... So like the thing that usually gets a below six figures or a low six figures earner in any business up into high six figures is the thing you know we teach to everybody all the time and beating it up at least 25 years is the quantum leap is shifting from being the doer of your thing to the marketer of your thing, right? And that's true as far as that will carry you. And it will carry you pretty good way. I mean, most people locked into relatively low incomes regardless of their level of expertise or excellence that they deliver are stuck there because their view of their business is the doing of the thing. The cooking of the food, the cracking of the back, the fixing of the tooth, the waxing of the car, the styling of the hair, the whatever. And when you get them shifted out of that so that they're actually now in the marketing of that thing, that's a pretty good income leap. But truth be told, it has its limit because the focus is still on the thing, not on the greatest possible point of differentiation, which is the status of the individual providing the thing. And increasingly, all other options for differentiation are becoming harder and harder and harder and harder to use and sustain. And so this really is pretty important. And as I say, personally, I never liked it. So like my first conversation with Halbert was, I don't like it. And, you know, I mean, so like for many years, like we got that bull photograph plastered everywhere, right? For many years, if you go back and find a bunch of my early stuff, you don't find my picture anywhere. Because I mean, I still like having my photograph taken. But I think the camera steals your soul or something. But I mean, I didn't want it anywhere. I just said, I'm on a no part of this. And I was perfectly happy to sell somebody else and leave me alone. And, you know, Gary said, it doesn't matter whether you like it or not. It's completely irrelevant. The question is, how much money do you want? That's the question. And it's sort of like, you know, do you like exercise, right? Or do you like eating these foods? You don't have to like them. It's just how long you want to live. That's, you know, the deal, right? And so I think a shift for, and my observation, many of you I know, many of you in the room, I don't know, but many of you I know. And many of you stop a long way short of making your business about being in the business of you. You are still in the business of marketing your thing rather than being in the business of you. I give you a little cartoon.
